cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking why different colors of light were used in the film Titanic. The answer options are A, B, C, D. Looking back at the given text, it says that in Titanic, the lower class people are shown in warm-colored light that gives them a sense of life and happiness, while the rich are shown in flat light which portrays hardness and coldness. The text also mentions that this use of lighting is to show the issue of class segregation and to create stereotypes. The main characters breaking free of these stereotypes is the solution to the segregation problem. So the main reason given here is to highlight the difference between the rich and poor through lighting. So option A says "They were used to show the difference between the rich and the poor." That matches the explanation in the text. Option B says the director wanted good visual effects. While visual effects are part of it, the text specifically points out the thematic reason (class segregation) rather than just aesthetics. Option C talks about past representation of social status with light color. The text doesn't mention anything about how it was done in the past; it's about the specific use in this film.
